Subject: Cutting Bake Me Happy's Toast delivery costs
Body HTML:
Hey there,

I wanted to reach out because I noticed Bake Me Happy has delivery set up through Toast.

If you are using Toast Delivery Services, Shipday is worth comparing because we plug into the same Toast ordering flow, but give restaurants more control over the delivery experience.

Customers still order from your Toast site. Orders still come into Toast the same way. Shipday just powers the delivery side in the background: driver dispatch, branded tracking, customer updates, and delivery coordination.

The main difference is flexibility. Restaurants use Shipday to improve delivery coverage, create a better branded tracking experience, reduce reliance on one fulfillment option, and keep more control over what happens after the order leaves the restaurant.

We already work with Ohio brands like Romeo's and Zeppe's, and I thought there could be a similar fit for Bake Me Happy.

Worth 10-15 minutes to compare it against what you are using today? You can grab a time on my calendar.

Best,
Saad

Sequence: Shipday — Toast Pickup-Only (No Delivery AT ALL)
Subject: Adding delivery to One Line Coffee's Toast site
Body HTML:
Hey Zach,

I wanted to reach out because I noticed One Line Coffee has Toast online ordering set up for pickup, but I did not see direct delivery available.

That can be a missed revenue lever. If customers already know your food but want it delivered, they either go to DoorDash or Uber Eats, call the restaurant, or just order somewhere else.

Shipday plugs into Toast so One Line Coffee can offer delivery directly from your existing Toast ordering page without hiring drivers or changing the store workflow.

Orders still come into Toast the same way. Shipday just handles the delivery side in the background: dispatching a driver to your restaurant, sending branded live tracking, updating the customer, and coordinating the drop-off.

The main idea is simple: turn your existing Toast site from pickup-only into a direct pickup + delivery channel.

We already work with Ohio brands like Romeo's and Zeppe's, and I thought there could be a similar fit for One Line Coffee.

Worth 10-15 minutes to see how this would plug into your Toast setup? You can grab a time on my calendar.
